creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to Philippe Audeoud from comment #2)
> Hello,
> We will discuss to remove spamhauss from our RBL.
> 
> Regards,

Firstly, I'd like to apologize for the "emotional outburst".
This is *not* the way to deal with issues. Sorry.
I'm afraid it was quite late, at the end of a *long* day,
and I [unwittingly] took the bounce personally. Foolish, I
know. Again, sorry.

To the point. My issue was not so much toward FreeBSD's
handling of bounces, as to the policies of the RBL FreeBSD
had chosen. Case in point; The ~8,190 IP address were assigned
to me ~10 years ago. Since that time, not one of those IP addresses
have been responsible for a single case of abuse. This is no
small undertaking, when you consider the fact that these IP's
are managed solely by myself -- which added to my frustration
for being bounced.
In the end, I managed to find a link to correct the issue at
the Spamhaus' website.
I realize that this policy/neglect is not that of FreeBSD, but
of Spamhaus. But by virtue of FreeBSD using them, they are
subject to/of their neglect.

Regardless of *which* RBL FreeBSD should choose to make their
MX's less subject to SPAM. May I request that FreeBSD provide as
part of the bounce message, a link to the RBL's "resolution process"?
So as to give the recipient of the bounce mail, better recourse
for their issue?

Thank you for all your time, and consideration, and
once again, sorry for the original noise.

--Chris
